title: fraxinus oxycarpa subsp. syriaca
intro: Fraxinus oxycarpa subsp. syriaca, also known as the Syrian ash or white ash, is a subspecies of the Fraxinus oxycarpa tree native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. The bark of this plant contains salicin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it a potential source of medicinal properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus oxycarpa subsp. syriaca include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the bark is typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or salves, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential and ensure safe use.
